Typed Semantic Definitions Refactor

One-off refactor living doc for making living-doc structure and behavior executable without moving concrete docs out of JSON.

Doc ID doc:typed-semantic-definitions-refactor
Title Typed Semantic Definitions Refactor
Scope Living Doc Compositor registry, templates, MCP diagnostics, skills, generated docs, and contract tests.
Owner / Team Living Docs
Generated 2026-05-06T00:28:55.009Z
Version / Revision draft
Location URL Unknown
Team Unknown
Canonical Origin docs/typed-semantic-definitions-refactor.json
Derived From Unknown
Source Coverage Seeded from GitHub issue #163, tracked as an epic with subtickets #164-#168, and updated with completed #164 real-run proof plus #167 OSS issue template coverage on 2026-05-06.

Objective Integrate typed semantic definitions into Living Doc Compositor so living-doc structure and behavior have one executable source that generates the registry/template artifacts, relationship graphs, MCP diagnostics, tests, and reference diagrams while concrete living doc instances remain JSON.

Success condition The refactor is complete when typed definitions are the maintained executable source for the intended living-doc semantic machinery, generate the selected registry/template artifacts deterministically, preserve existing JSON/HTML rendering and sync flows, drive MCP relationship/stage diagnostics and valid operations, support skill use through concrete patchable operations, and have contract tests that prevent drift. Individual implementation slices are checkpoints toward this full integration, not alternative objectives or completion claims.

Status Snapshot surface2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Two gates complete; remaining gates explicit

#164 is satisfied by the real-run semantic patch proof, and #167 is satisfied by adding typed coverage for oss-issue-deep-dive.

oss-issue-deep-dive was selected because its workflow has durable relationships: symptoms localize to code anchors, anchors are tested by attempts, attempts produce findings, and orbit/stance constrain the repair path.

The remaining completion gates are #165 generation boundary, #166 compositor UI/readability, and #168 final completion proof.

Issue

#163

Closed gates

#164, #167

Typed templates

4

First PR target

surface-delivery

Stage

Coverage expanded

Formal Model surface2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Typed semantic definitions as source of machinery

2026-05-05T08:10:51Z
Specified
Primitives convergence-type template relationship stage-signal valid-operation
Invariants doc-instances-stay-json generated-artifacts-readable
Authorities issue-163 code-representation-page
Code scripts/living-doc-registry.json docs/living-doc-template-surface-delivery.json scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs scripts/sync-compositor-embeds.mjs
Tickets
#163

This model does not replace concrete living doc JSON instances.

The first definition source should be additive and prove value on one template family.

Definition Reusable living-doc semantic machinery is authored as typed definitions for types, templates, expected relationships, stage signals, diagnostics, and operations; generated JSON/metadata remain the artifacts consumed by the compositor, renderer, MCP server, tests, and docs.

Authority scope Registry/template semantics, template relationship graphs, MCP stage diagnostics, valid operations, generated prompt context, and generated diagrams.

Violations (2)

Moving concrete doc content into code would break the artifact boundary.

Generating artifacts that are not deterministic, readable, or compatible with the compositor would fail the refactor objective.

Generated template relationship graph

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Implemented
Primitives template relationship stage-signal valid-operation
Invariants prove-before-expanding
Authorities graph-mcp-model-page issue-163
Code scripts/generated/living-doc-template-graphs.json scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs
Tickets
#163

The generated graph includes machine-readable stage signal conditions, not only explanatory when text.

MCP evaluates those conditions generically for current docs rather than hardcoding template-specific stage logic.

The model now covers four different families: surface delivery, proof/canonicality, operations support, and OSS issue deep-dive.

Definition A generated graph captures template section order, expected type relationships, stage signals, and valid operations so MCP can reason over relationships rather than only local section shape.

Authority scope Read-only MCP diagnostics and generated documentation for template relationship maps.

Model Assertions act2026-05-05T12:17:40.631Z

The full integration objective is settled

2026-05-05T12:04:19.944Z
Canonical
Primitives typed-semantic-definition generated-artifact mcp-diagnostic valid-operation
Invariants objective-is-settled doc-instances-stay-json
Authorities issue-163 code-representation-page graph-mcp-model-page
Tickets
#163

The refactor is not exploring whether typed definitions should become executable semantic machinery; that direction is already the objective.

The implementation questions are sequencing and proof questions: which type/template surface to migrate next, which generated artifacts become canonical next, how MCP and skills use the generated behavior, and which contracts prove the system is integrated.

Small implementation slices should be recorded as checkpoints toward this objective, not as separate strategies or endpoints.

JSON is still the correct representation for stored living doc state because it is portable, inspectable, patchable, renderable, and archival.

This is the first concrete step where code representation carries behavior, not only documentation or generated metadata.

Design-Code-Spec Flow surface2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Full refactor integration path

Typed semantic definitionsIntegration Path2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
In Progress Partial
Code refs scripts/living-doc-definitions scripts/generated/living-doc-template-graphs.json scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs scripts/render-living-doc.mjs tests/contract
UX spec refs #issue-163 #code-representation-page #graph-mcp-model-page
Tickets
#163#164#165#166#167#168

The destination is full integration: typed definitions author the selected semantic structure and behavior, generated artifacts stay deterministic and readable, MCP consumes the generated semantics, skills can operate from the diagnostics, and JSON remains the document instance format.

Current completed checkpoints include typed definitions for four template families, generated graphs and diagrams, generated semantic-definition blocks embedded into selected template JSON artifacts, generated semantic-use blocks embedded into registry convergence types, semantic context in rendered snapshots, relationship and stage diagnostics, repair operations, patch drafts, patch-draft application proof, contract coverage, the #164 real-run semantic patch proof, and the #167 OSS issue deep-dive coverage expansion.

Remaining checkpoints are now split into GitHub subtickets: #165 generation boundary, #166 semantic UI/readability surface, and #168 final completion proof.

Do not stop after very small slices unless the slice clearly advances one of those subtickets or closes it with explicit deferral rationale.

First PR: additive surface-delivery template graph

Relationship and stage MCP tools

2026-05-05T11:57:26.554Z
Current
Tickets
#163

Path scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs

Line living_doc_template_diagrams / living_doc_relationship_gaps / living_doc_stage_diagnostics / living_doc_valid_stage_operations

Revision working tree

What it does Exposes generated template graph and diagram metadata, compares living docs to expected section and card-level relationships, and returns relationship gaps, repair operations, likely stage candidates, and valid operations.

Why it matters (5)

This is the first point where typed semantic definitions produce inference-time behavior and inspectable representation rather than only a generated artifact.

Relationship diagnostics now distinguish populated-but-unlinked cards from genuinely connected cards where evidence rules exist.

Relationship gaps now include generated repair operations so skills can move directly from diagnosis to a bounded next action.

Relationship gaps now include minimal living-doc-ai-patch/v1 drafts for direct card-create repairs where the target section exists.

Patch drafts include validation results from the existing patch validator so a skill can distinguish an immediately applicable draft from one that needs editing.

Current
Tickets
#163

Path .agents/skills/living-doc/SKILL.md

Revision working tree

What it does Requires the bootstrap living-doc skill to inspect generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid stage operations, and validated patch drafts when a document matches a generated template graph.

Why it matters (2)

This makes the generated semantic machinery part of the operator workflow instead of leaving it as an optional MCP surface.

The skill now treats patch drafts as reviewed operation proposals that must be validated and applied through the patch tools.

Updated the bootstrap living-doc skill and complex inference harness skill to inspect generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, and reviewed patch drafts.

Added a contract test that prevents those skills from dropping the semantic tool path.

What it proved (1)

The generated semantic MCP surface is now part of the instructed skill workflow rather than a sidecar tool an agent may ignore.

Apply a generated relationship patch draft

2026-05-05T12:35:36.874Z
Probe
Tickets
#163

The applied draft creates an alignment card linked with sourceCardIds: ["primary-surface"].

After application, flow-feeds-alignment no longer reports missing-target-cards or missing-card-evidence, and stage diagnostics move from Coherence toward the next Operation gap.

Shipped in working tree

Cost to apply low

What was tried (2)

Extended the MCP contract so a generated flow-feeds-alignment patch draft is applied to a surface-delivery living doc fixture through living_doc_patch_apply.

Re-ran relationship gaps and stage diagnostics after application.

What it proved (1)

The generated semantic path can move from diagnosis to valid operation to patch draft to applied JSON update, and the original relationship gap disappears after application.

Generate semantic uses into selected registry types

2026-05-05T12:17:40.631Z
Probe
Tickets
#163

tests/contract/registry-semantic-uses.spec.mjs checks that generated registry semantic-use blocks match the template graph.

The generator currently annotates 13 convergence types used by surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, and operations-support.

Shipped in working tree

Cost to apply medium

What was tried (2)

Added a registry semantic-use generator that annotates convergence types used by typed template families with generated template roles, relationships, stage signals, and graph provenance.

Synced the generated registry metadata into docs/scripts/living-doc-registry.json and the embedded compositor registry.

What it proved (1)

The selected registry artifact can now participate in the typed-definition generation chain, not only the template JSON artifacts and MCP sidecar graph.

Split remaining refactor work into epic subtickets

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Probe
Tickets
#163#164#165#166#167#168

#164 tracked the real-run semantic patch workflow proof and is now satisfied by docs/issue-164-semantic-patch-run.json.

#167 tracked typed coverage beyond the first three template families and is now satisfied by oss-issue-deep-dive coverage.

#165 tracks the template/registry generation boundary.

#166 tracks UI inspection for generated semantics.

#168 tracks final epic completion proof.

Shipped in GitHub issues #163-#168

Cost to apply low

What was tried (1)

Converted #163 into the parent epic and created five subtickets for the remaining completion items.

What it proved (1)

The remaining work is now tracked as concrete completion gates rather than vague open decisions.

Prove semantic patch workflow on a real living doc

2026-05-06T00:32:16Z
Ready
Tickets
#163#164

Before application, MCP reported flow-feeds-alignment as missing-target-cards and offered add-alignment-row as the repair operation.

The applied patch created semantic-patch-workflow-feeds with sourceCardIds: ["semantic-patch-workflow"].

After application, flow-feeds-alignment was no longer present in relationship gaps; one later-stage gap remained, which is acceptable because #164 only targets removal of the original gap.

Shipped in docs/issue-164-semantic-patch-run.json

Cost to apply medium

What was tried (3)

Created a real repo living doc whose section sequence lets MCP infer the generated surface-delivery template.

Ran living_doc_semantic_context, living_doc_relationship_gaps, living_doc_stage_diagnostics, living_doc_valid_stage_operations, living_doc_patch_validate, living_doc_patch_apply, and living_doc_render against that document.

Rendered the updated document after the generated patch created the missing alignment carrier card.

What it proved (3)

The original flow-feeds-alignment relationship gap disappeared after the generated add-alignment-row patch draft was applied to JSON.

Stage diagnostics advanced from Coherence to Judgment after application, showing that the generated relationship graph changed inference-time assessment rather than only editing content.

The same path can operate on a real living doc artifact, not only the contract-test fixture.

Expand typed coverage to OSS issue deep-dive

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Ready
Tickets
#163#167

oss-issue-deep-dive was selected before monitoring/competitor templates because its internal relationships are concrete and patchable inside one issue handoff: symptom -> anchor -> attempt -> finding, with issue orbit and maintainer stance as constraints.

npm run test:contract passes with four typed templates after the expansion.

Shipped in scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/oss-issue-deep-dive.mjs

Cost to apply medium

What was tried (3)

Added a typed semantic definition for oss-issue-deep-dive with six sections, five relationships, five stage signals, and five valid operations.

Generated graph, Mermaid diagram, template semantic-definition block, registry semantic-use blocks, embedded compositor data, and rendered template HTML.

Extended contracts so graph, diagram, and MCP diagnostics cover the new template family.

What it proved (3)

The typed-definition pipeline now covers four template families instead of three.

MCP can infer oss-issue-deep-dive, diagnose missing symptom-localized-by-anchor relationships, produce a valid add-code-anchor patch draft, apply it, and advance stage diagnostics to the next operational gap.

Generated registry semantic uses now annotate 18 convergence types used by typed template families.

Proof Ladder act2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Completion proof spans the whole integration path

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Partial
Tickets
#163#164#165#166#167#168

The refactor is not complete just because one diagnostic, diagram, or patch draft works.

Completion requires proof that typed definitions are the maintained source for the selected semantic machinery, generated artifacts are deterministic, selected template and registry artifacts carry generated semantic blocks, MCP behavior uses that generated source, skills are instructed to act on the output, generated patch drafts can be applied to update JSON, rendered docs remain standalone, and contract tests catch drift.

#164 and #167 are satisfied; the remaining proof is now distributed across #165, #166, and #168, with #168 responsible for the final completed/deferred/residual-risk judgment.

Selected template artifacts carry generated semantics

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Ready
Tickets
#163

node scripts/generate-living-doc-template-artifacts.mjs --check verifies generated semantic-definition blocks on all typed template families.

The selected template JSON artifacts now contain templateMeta.semanticDefinition with sections, relationships, stage signals, valid operations, counts, generated graph metadata, and generated Mermaid diagram source.

Four selected template HTML snapshots now carry generated semantic context after render, including oss-issue-deep-dive.

Selected registry types carry generated semantic uses

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Ready
Tickets
#163

node scripts/generate-living-doc-registry-semantics.mjs --check verifies generated semantic-use blocks on all convergence types used by typed template families.

The selected registry entries now contain semanticUses with template roles, incoming and outgoing relationships, related stage signals, and graph artifact provenance.

node scripts/sync-compositor-embeds.mjs mirrors those generated registry semantics into the standalone compositor and docs/scripts registry.

The generator now annotates 18 convergence types used by surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive.

Tickets
#163

tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs now proves living_doc_relationship_gaps -> patchDraft -> living_doc_patch_validate -> living_doc_patch_apply -> living_doc_relationship_gaps on a generated surface-delivery doc fixture.

The proof confirms that applying the generated draft changes the JSON document and resolves the original relationship gap.

The previous weaker part, exercising the same path in a non-fixture ongoing living-doc session, is now covered by the #164 proof document.

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Tickets
#163

living_doc_relationship_gaps detects missing or weak expected relationships from generated template graph metadata.

living_doc_stage_diagnostics derives stage candidates from machine-readable condition objects in the generated graph.

Contracts now prove section-level gaps for surface-delivery (flow-feeds-alignment), proof-canonicality (assertion-requires-proof), operations-support (operation-routes-surface), and oss-issue-deep-dive (symptom-localized-by-anchor).

Contracts now also prove card-level evidence gaps for the first three families and patch application for the OSS issue symptom-to-anchor repair.

living_doc_semantic_context can retrieve this payload from rendered HTML snapshots.

#163

Decision Record act2026-05-05T13:02:59.836Z

The objective is full typed semantic integration

2026-05-05T12:04:19.944Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#163

The direction is settled: typed semantic definitions should become the executable source for living-doc semantic structure and behavior.

Remaining work should be described as implementation checkpoints and proof gates, not as open strategic choices.

A small slice is useful only when it advances the full integration path.

Do not move living doc instances out of JSON

2026-05-05T08:10:51Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#163

Typed code definitions are for semantic machinery; concrete living docs remain JSON artifacts.

Start with additive generated graph metadata

2026-05-05T08:10:51Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#163

The first PR should not rewrite the registry or existing template JSON.

Use surface-delivery as the first proof family unless implementation reveals a better fit

2026-05-05T08:10:51Z
Reference
Tickets
#163

Surface delivery has a clear relationship chain and should expose whether generated graph diagnostics add practical value.

Track remaining work as epic subtickets

2026-05-05T13:02:59.836Z
Ground Truth
Tickets
#163#164#165#166#167#168

The full integration objective remains settled; the subtickets are execution gates, not alternative directions.

Closing #163 requires either closing the subtickets or explicitly recording deferred scope in #168 and this living doc.

Tooling Surface surface2026-05-06T00:45:00Z

Semantic graph generator

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Prototype
Scripts scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs scripts/living-doc-definitions/define.mjs scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/surface-delivery.mjs scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/proof-canonicality.mjs scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/operations-support.mjs scripts/living-doc-definitions/templates/oss-issue-deep-dive.mjs
Tooling artifacts scripts/generated/living-doc-template-graphs.json scripts/generated/living-doc-template-diagrams.json
Tickets
#163

Purpose Generate template relationship graph metadata from typed semantic definitions.

When to use Run after changing typed definitions, and in contract tests to verify generated artifact determinism.

Invocation node scripts/generate-living-doc-semantic-graph.mjs

Caveats and gaps (6)

Covers surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive so far.

Generates graph and diagram metadata; scripts/generate-living-doc-template-artifacts.mjs writes selected semantic-definition blocks into the matching template JSON artifacts, and scripts/generate-living-doc-registry-semantics.mjs writes semantic-use blocks into the registry types used by those templates.

Relationship evidence currently supports the shared-field-value rule shape across the typed families.

Relationships can name repairOperationIds that resolve to valid operation definitions in MCP gap output.

Relationship gaps can produce minimal living-doc-ai-patch/v1 draft repairs from the same evidence rule and operation metadata.

Stage conditions are intentionally small and structural: section-empty, related-relationship-gap, source-populated-target-empty, all-populated-no-high-gaps, and manual-review.

Template graph contract test

2026-05-06T00:45:00Z
Trusted
Scripts tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs tests/contract/template-diagrams.spec.mjs tests/contract/template-artifacts.spec.mjs tests/contract/registry-semantic-uses.spec.mjs tests/contract/skills-semantic-tools.spec.mjs tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs tests/contract/render.spec.mjs
Tickets
#163

Purpose Ensure generated graph, diagram, selected template artifact metadata, selected registry semantic-use metadata, and semantic-aware skill instructions are deterministic, reference valid registry/template types and relationships, are exposed through MCP, travel with rendered matching docs, and can be retrieved from rendered snapshots across the typed template families.

When to use Run as part of npm run test:contract after the generator exists.

Invocation node tests/contract/template-graphs.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/template-diagrams.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/template-artifacts.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/registry-semantic-uses.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/skills-semantic-tools.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/mcp-server.spec.mjs && node tests/contract/render.spec.mjs

Caveats and gaps (10)

Validates deterministic generation and registry/template references for surface-delivery, proof-canonicality, operations-support, and oss-issue-deep-dive.

Validates generated semantic-definition blocks embedded into the selected template JSON artifacts.

Validates generated semantic-use blocks embedded into the selected registry convergence types.

Validates the core skills reference generated semantic context, relationship gaps, stage diagnostics, valid operations, patch draft review, patch validation, and patch application.

Validates relationship evidence rule shape in the generated graph.

Validates relationship repair operation references against each template's valid operations.

Validates rendered semantic context embedding on surface-delivery.

Validates MCP semantic context retrieval from both source JSON and rendered HTML.

Validates MCP relationship gap detection, repair operation output, patch draft shape, embedded patch validation, patch draft validation, patch draft application, and post-apply gap resolution for generated relationship gaps.

Will need stronger assertions as generated diagnostics move from section/card population toward richer card-level semantic edges.

Existing living-doc MCP server

2026-05-05T10:16:32.342Z
Usable With Caveats
Scripts scripts/living-doc-mcp-server.mjs
Tickets
#163

Purpose Expose living-doc registry, structure, source, coverage, governance, patch, and render operations to inference-time agents.

When to use Use as the integration point for generated relationship/stage metadata.

Invocation npm run ldoc:mcp

Caveats and gaps (3)

Now exposes living_doc_template_graph, living_doc_template_diagrams, living_doc_semantic_context, living_doc_relationship_gaps, living_doc_stage_diagnostics, and living_doc_valid_stage_operations.

living_doc_structure_reflect includes semantic graph metadata for matching docs.

Relationship diagnostics validate shared-field card evidence where definitions provide it; broader edge semantics are still intentionally unsupported until needed.

Board

Columns are generated from registry status sets; cards are grouped from convergence-type status fields.
